name: INSTITUTE FOR SOCIAL POLICY AND UNDERSTANDING
mission: ISPU PROVIDES OBJECTIVE RESEARCH AND EDUCATION ABOUT AMERICAN MUSLIMS TO SUPPORT WELL-INFORMED DIALOGUE AND DECISION-MAKING. ISPU IS A NONPARTISAN, NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION THAT BUILDS UNDERSTANDING AND STRENGTHENS COMMUNITIES BY LAYING A FOUNDATION OF FACTS.
theoryOfChange: Relevant, rigorous research in the right hands strengthens Muslim communities and equips those working toward full and equitable inclusion; ISPU discovers new data through objective research, educates the public through accessible resources and events, and equips leaders with toolkits and materials so the data is applied by others to catalyze change.
scoreSummary: INSTITUTE FOR SOCIAL POLICY AND UNDERSTANDING shows good impact and good alignment, with zakat compliance.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows a medium donor fit for those interested in advocacy and research-based causes [4]. It maintains a solid track record in the American Muslim space and offers clear zakat-eligibility guidelines for contributors [2][9].
dimension_explanations.impact: ISPU demonstrates effectiveness through capacity building, training institutions on research and equipping policymakers with data [5][6]. While the program expense ratio is 72.9%, the cost per beneficiary is relatively high at 1870.318, which is typical for organizations focused on high-level advocacy and systemic change rather than direct service [1][3].

Efficient fundraising operations spending only $0.19 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: founded in 2002, the Institute for Social Policy and Understanding (ISPU) conducts rigorous research to equip decision-makers and inform the public through evidence-based data [4][9]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing donors to support its mission of community education and policy research through religious giving [2].

alignment.improvement: Clarify the specific zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories to provide more detail for religiously motivated donors.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust ISPU due to its <cite id="11">98.25/100 from Charity Navigator Accountability score</cite> and its <cite id="10">Platinum Candid Seal</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility further, the organization could publish more detailed board meeting minutes and specific conflict-of-interest disclosures.

impact.improvement: If reserves remain at <cite id="12">22.6 months</cite>, the charity should document specific triggers for deploying these funds into new research initiatives.

rich_narrative.headline: The Institute for Social Policy and Understanding provides objective research and education to foster well-informed decision-making regarding American Muslims.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value data-driven advocacy and want to fund the intellectual infrastructure necessary to protect and empower American Muslims.
donor_motivations: Want to support evidence-based research over emotional appeals
Care about long-term policy influence and media representation
Value high transparency and third-party accountability ratings
Seek to fulfill zakat through community-wide educational empowerment
giving_considerations: Impact is measured in influence and education, not meals or kits
Research cycles are multi-year and require sustained funding
Organization has a very strong cash position, suggesting high stability
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking immediate, tangible humanitarian relief or those who prefer local, direct-service charities like food banks.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects verified outcome tracking of institutional reach and media impact, though not RCT-level social change data.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: ISPU uses research to create a foundation of fact that equips change agents to improve the public narrative and policy environment.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Research impact is often invisible; tracking institutional training ensures the data is actually being used by leaders.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While stable, the organization has a high funding gap for expanding its research into new demographic areas.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by <cite id="1">20+ years of objective data collection</cite> and a Platinum transparency rating.

strengths.detail: ISPU holds a <cite id="11">98.25/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>.
The charity maintains <cite id="12">22.6 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring long-term stability for multi-year research projects.
The organization spends only <cite id="13">$0.17 to raise every $1</cite>, which is significantly better than industry averages.

strengths_deep_dive: ISPU demonstrates a high level of organizational maturity through its governance and financial management. The organization maintains a <cite id="14">100% independent board of directors</cite>, which provides critical oversight for its $1.6 million budget. Their financial health is further evidenced by a <cite id="11">perfect 98/100 score in Accountability & Finance</cite> from Charity Navigator. This fiscal discipline allows them to focus on high-impact outputs, such as their <cite id="15">567 media mentions</cite> that help shape the public narrative surrounding American Muslims.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2002, the Institute for Social Policy and Understanding (ISPU) provides <cite id="1">objective research and education about American Muslims</cite> to support well-informed dialogue. The organization operates as a research institution rather than a direct service provider, focusing on <cite id="2">equipping community change agents, media, and policymakers</cite> with evidence-based data. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $1,624,938</cite>, maintaining a stable financial trajectory with a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 7.7%</cite>. ISPU is uniquely positioned as a <cite id="5">zakat-eligible research organization</cite>, allowing donors to fulfill religious obligations by supporting the dissemination of knowledge. Their work is categorized under the <cite id="6">fisabilillah zakat category</cite>, focusing on the intellectual and social advancement of the community. The organization's impact is measured through its reach into influential sectors, having <cite id="7">trained 851 institutions</cite> and <cite id="8">equipped 1,539 policymakers and community leaders</cite> with research-backed toolkits. While the cost per beneficiary is higher than traditional relief charities at <cite id="9">$1,870.32 per beneficiary</cite>, this reflects the intensive nature of high-level research and capacity-building efforts. ISPU maintains high standards of transparency, holding a <cite id="10">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and an <cite id="11">overall score of 98.98.25/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>.
